summary: Stock entry speed improvement (Product Code grid view) tested end-to-end. Backend GET/PUT /api/admin/product-codes verified with pytest (8/8 passing). Frontend tested via Playwright: login → Product Codes page assigns codes → Grid View shows 100 boxes (1-100), assigned boxes are active/clickable while unassigned are greyed out → tapping an assigned box opens modal pre-filled with product name and current stock → save works. List View / Grid View toggle works correctly. Production data restored after testing.

minor.issue: Destructive semantics: every PUT first $unsets product_code on ALL products before applying the supplied mappings. The frontend already always sends the FULL list of currently-assigned codes, so this is correct behavior for a bulk-replace API, but it is risky if any future caller sends a partial list — they will silently wipe every other code. Consider documenting this as 'replace-all' or adding a separate granular endpoint.

issues: Inactive (unassigned) grid boxes use a very low-contrast grey number on a pale beige tile (e.g. '#bdbdbd' on '#f0e8d8'). On bright mobile screens this is barely readable. Consider darker number color or stronger 'disabled' visual treatment.
When there are no unsaved changes the green 'Save' button still appears prominent (only background changes to grey on disabled). On the initial load (0 changes) the button looks active at a glance.

testability.issue: All `data-testid` props in /app/frontend/app/(admin)/product-codes.tsx (code-input-*) and /app/frontend/app/(admin)/manage-stock.tsx (grid-view-btn, list-view-btn, grid-box-*, grid-stock-input, grid-stock-save) are NOT propagated to the DOM by React Native Web. document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid]') returns []. To make the app testable in browser/Playwright, the components should pass `dataSet={{ testid: 'xyz' }}` (RN Web converts to data-testid) OR wrap with View/Pressable that supports the prop, OR use `accessibilityLabel`. This blocks reliable Playwright automation across the app.

action_items: (Optional) Improve grid-box-disabled contrast — text color #bdbdbd is too light on the pale-beige tile background.

(Optional) Make `data-testid` props actually reach the DOM on React Native Web (use dataSet={{ testid }} or a helper). Currently zero data-testid attributes appear in the rendered DOM, making automated testing brittle (text-based selectors required).
(Optional) Add a note/comment in the backend PUT handler clarifying that the call is a destructive 'replace-all' so future callers don't accidentally wipe codes by sending a partial list.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend PUT /api/admin/product-codes performs a global $unset across ALL products on every call — relies entirely on the frontend always sending the complete mapping list. Safe today but fragile.
GET /api/admin/product-codes correctly projects out `_id` (no Mongo ObjectId leakage).

Frontend product-codes.tsx: validation limits codes to 1-100 and detects duplicates client-side, but there is NO backend uniqueness validation. A malicious / buggy client could PUT duplicate codes; backend would happily store them and the Grid View would only display one of them. Consider validating unique codes server-side.
manage-stock.tsx is 1131 lines — consider splitting Grid View, List View, and modal into separate components (exceeds the 700-line guideline).

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows (login, Grid/List toggle, Product Codes assign+save, Grid box click → modal → pre-filled stock value) work as expected

seed_data_creation: During testing, temporarily assigned product_code=1 to 'Brioche burger buns' and product_code=2 to 'Jumbo Brioche loaf'. ALL test mappings were cleared at the end via PUT [] — verified Coded=0/109 on the production DB after teardown. Backend pytest fixtures also snapshot+restore in case of future re-runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend endpoints GET/PUT /api/admin/product-codes are fully verified (test_product_codes.py). They require admin Bearer token. PUT body shape: [{product_id, product_code}]. The endpoint is DESTRUCTIVE — it unsets all product_code fields, then applies the supplied list. Frontend RN Web does NOT propagate `data-testid` to DOM — use text selectors (e.g. 'text=Grid View', placeholder='--', placeholder='Enter stock') for any Playwright tests. Manage Stock Grid View renders exactly 100 fixed-size boxes (1-100); boxes with an assigned product become clickable and a modal opens with product name + numeric stock input.

backend_pytest.TestUpdateProductCodes::test_put_assigns: PASS (codes 1, 50, 100 assigned to 3 products and verified via GET)

frontend_playwright_flows.boxes_1_and_2_become_active_others_greye: PASS (box 1 shows stock 73, box 2 shows '-', boxes 3-100 disabled)
frontend_playwright_flows.click_active_box_1_opens_modal: PASS (modal shows '#1 Brioche burger buns' and input pre-filled with 73, Save+Cancel buttons visible)
frontend_playwright_flows.list_view_continues_to_work: PASS (List View still renders 148 products with +/- stock controls)

rca_of_the_issue: N/A — feature works as specified. Only finding worth flagging is testability: React Native Web does not forward the `data-testid` prop to the underlying DOM. To make selectors reliable in Playwright, the codebase should use `dataSet={{ testid: 'foo' }}` (which RN Web converts to `data-testid='foo'`) rather than `data-testid='foo'` directly.
